summary: Matched several vi0502 and vi0601 cutscene/VI functions by adding C implementations, renaming discovered symbols, and refining supporting baselib/grlib types. The decomp.dev report credited 5 new full matches totaling 692 bytes of matched code and an 81.67% improvement for vi0502_8031E124. The PR also introduced a VecMtx/VecMtxPtr type in baselib/mtx because grLib_801C9A10 is accessed as a matrix-like array of Vec3 rows from vi0502.
what_changed: Added a 134-line vi0502.c implementation covering vi0502_8031E124, vi0502_8031E304, vi0502_8031E328, and vi0502_RunFrame.
Added vi0502.h prototypes for vi0502_8031E124, vi0502_8031E304, vi0502_8031E328, and vi0502_RunFrame.
Added vi0601_CameraCallback and vi0601_RunFrame to vi0601.c, plus a vi0601_RunFrame declaration in vi0601.h.

Renamed symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt and asm references: un_8031E124 to vi0502_8031E124, fn_8031E304 to vi0502_8031E304, fn_8031E328 to vi0502_8031E328, fn_8031E3BC to vi0502_RunFrame, un_8031E710 to vi0601_CameraCallback, and fn_8031EB38 to vi0601_RunFrame.
Renamed sbss objects un_804D6FA0 to erase_colors_vi0502, un_804D6FA4 to kirby_gobj, and un_804D6FB4 to erase_colors_vi0601.

Changed grLib_8049EF58 from static Vec3[6] to static VecMtx and split the trailing storage into grLib_unusedvec1 and grLib_unusedvec2, reflected in symbols.txt as grLib_8049EF58 size 0x30 plus two 0xC unused vectors.
Changed grLib_801C9A10 return type from Vec3* to VecMtxPtr in grlib.c and grlib.h.
Added typedef Vec3 VecMtx[4] and typedef Vec3* VecMtxPtr to src/sysdolphin/baselib/mtx.h.

Added HSD_JObjGetTranslation2 inline helper in jobj.h with only the jobj assert, documented as likely fake but needed by vi0502 because the observed usage lacks the second assert from HSD_JObjGetTranslation.
Updated remaining asm label references to the new vi0502/vi0601 and erase_colors/kirby_gobj names.
smart_moves: Used symbol renaming to make remaining assembly and C implementation agree on newly understood roles, especially erase color globals and callback/run-frame names.
Modeled grLib_801C9A10 as VecMtxPtr instead of plain Vec3*, matching the observed vi0502 access pattern pmtx[1] and preserving the backing BSS layout with explicit unused Vec3 slots.
Introduced a separate HSD_JObjGetTranslation2 helper rather than forcing the existing HSD_JObjGetTranslation inline when the assert pattern did not match.
Matched callback-style functions with descriptive names based on usage: vi0601_CameraCallback is passed to GObj_SetupGXLinkMax, and vi0601_RunFrame is passed to HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54.
Kept the decomp work scoped: fully matched small animation/camera callbacks while leaving vi0502_8031E124 as an improved but not fully matched function.
decomp_lessons: When an existing inline helper has extra asserts that disturb matching, a narrowly scoped duplicate inline with a TODO can document the mismatch and unblock a function.
BSS object boundaries may need to be reinterpreted when a function reveals a more specific aggregate type; here Vec3[6] became VecMtx plus two unused Vec3 objects.
Use callsite role to name callbacks: functions installed through GX link setup or GObj proc setup can often be named CameraCallback or RunFrame even before all surrounding code is matched.
Erase color globals are often four-byte GXColor-like objects accessed byte-by-byte before HSD_SetEraseColor; renaming them helps clarify camera erase-screen routines.
Partial matches are still useful when they force better types and symbol names that enable adjacent small functions to reach 100%.
naming_conventions: For unknown vi submodule functions with known file ownership but unclear semantic name, use the file prefix plus address, e.g. vi0502_8031E124.
Use file-prefixed semantic names for understood callbacks and frame procs, e.g. vi0502_RunFrame, vi0601_CameraCallback, and vi0601_RunFrame.
Name small sbss color globals by purpose and file scope, e.g. erase_colors_vi0502 and erase_colors_vi0601.
Name a discovered player entity global by its content when clear from Player_GetEntity usage, e.g. kirby_gobj.
Explicitly name split unused BSS vectors as grLib_unusedvec1 and grLib_unusedvec2 when preserving layout after tightening the main object type.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Updated asm labels and symbol table entries in lockstep with C names to keep cross-file references coherent.
Matched HSD_CObj camera callbacks by reproducing HSD_CObjSetCurrent, HSD_SetEraseColor, HSD_CObjEraseScreen, gxlink_prios assignments, HSD_GObj_80390ED0 calls, and HSD_CObjEndCurrent order.

Matched run-frame routines by animating the CObj first, comparing current frame to literal float trigger frames, firing vi_8031C9B4/audio actions, then ending via lb_800145F4 and gm_801A4B60 at end_frame.
Used GET_JOBJ and GET_COBJ access macros to express object field access in matching-friendly C.
Introduced VecMtxPtr so grLib_801C9A10 callers can index returned vectors as pmtx[1], aligning source shape with generated addressing.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The PR body explicitly justified adding VecMtx to baselib/mtx because it was more appropriate for grLib_801C9A10 based on how vi0502 accesses the returned data.
The decomp.dev bot reported 5 new full matches: vi0601_CameraCallback, vi0502_8031E328, vi0502_RunFrame, vi0601_RunFrame, and vi0502_8031E304.
The decomp.dev bot reported vi0502_8031E124 improved to 81.67% but not fully matched.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove or rename the likely fake HSD_JObjGetTranslation2 inline.
Search for other grLib_801C9A10 callers to validate VecMtxPtr and grLib_8049EF58 layout.
Look for subsequent full match work on vi0502_8031E124.
Search vi0502/vi0601 for additional erase_colors globals and run-frame callback naming patterns.
Check whether vi0601_CameraCallback should be declared in vi0601.h if used outside the C file.
